mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary overheating, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL move in opposite directions, or 20-day returns are negative in >55% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations during post-shock recovery phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 310.0, persistent inflationary pressure forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when inflation expectations are anchored below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and elevated federal funds rates creates a stagflationary risk profile where tight monetary policy clashes with sticky labor costs, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields positive 60-day returns in >55% of co-occurring instances, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from independent single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate renewed confidence in long-term economic stability and construction sector activity, triggering short-term sector rotation into equities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following HOUST increases during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive power when mortgage rates exceed 7.0%.
